title: Cryopreservation of microalgal cultures
steps: Make a 10 % (v/v) solution of DMSO in the appropriate culture medium for the strains being preserved. Eg. 45 mL, culture medium medium and 5 mL, DMSO to make 50 mL cryoprotectant. Work in the fume hood when using DMSO.
Prepare the cryotubes with a 1:1 ratio of cryoprotectant:culture Eg. 1 mL, cryoprotectant and 1 mL, culture for a 2 mL cryotube.
Filter sterilise the cryoprotectant using a 0.2 μM filter .
Once the cryoprotectant and culture have been mixed in the cryotubes, allow 00:10:00 for the cryoprotectant to penetrate.
Place the cryotubes into a Mr Frosty freezing container (filled to the line with isopropyl alcohol/isopropanol/propan-2-ol).
Place the freezing container in a -80 °C freezer Overnight .
Remove the tubes from the freezer and flash freeze them in liquid nitrogen.
Store the tubes either in liquid nitrogen or in a -150 °C freezer for as long as required.
To recover a cryopreserved culture, thaw the cryotubes in a water bath pre-heated to 40 °C to prevent ice crystals from re-forming as they thaw.
Add the defrosted culture to 20 mL, fresh culture medium .
Place the culture at the appropriate temperature but keep it in the dark for 24:00:00 , for example by wrapping the culture flask in aluminium foil.
After 24:00:00 , remove the aluminium foil. Monitor the culture to determine if recovery has been successful.
creator: Victoria LN Jackson
